steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The hypothesis predicts that paired leaves should tend to show opposite side orientation. Supporting data must confirm this pattern holds across the species tested — all three grapevine species.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'opposite side orientation is fairly high in the July grape, but not as high as it is in the graybark grape': only compares two species and says nothing about the frost grape. Incomplete support. ✗
  • Choice B — 'same side orientation is fairly high in the July grape': highlights same-side orientation, which is the opposite of what the hypothesis predicts. ✗
  • Choice C — 'all three species show more leaf pairs with opposite side orientations than with same-side orientations': confirms the predicted pattern (opposite > same) across all three species tested. ✓
  • Choice D — 'all the leaf pairs show opposite-side orientation': claims 100% prevalence, which the graph does not show and is implausibly absolute. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
